data.name: LuceneDev1000_AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Floating point types should not be compared for exact equality.
data.comment: An optional longer localizable description of the diagnostic.

data.name: LuceneDev1000_AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: '{0}' may fail due to JIT optimizations. Floating point types should not be compared for exact equality.
data.comment: The format-able message the diagnostic displays.

data.name: LuceneDev1000_AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Floating point types should not be compared for exact equality

data.name: LuceneDev1001_AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Floating point types should be formatted with J2N.Numerics.Single.ToString() or J2N.Numerics.Double.ToString().
data.comment: An optional longer localizable description of the diagnostic.

data.name: LuceneDev1001_AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: '{0}' may fail due to floating point precision issues on .NET Framework and .NET Core prior to version 3.0. Floating point types should be formatted with J2N.Numerics.Single.ToString() or J2N.Numerics.Double.ToString().
data.comment: The format-able message the diagnostic displays.

data.name: LuceneDev1001_AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Floating point types should be formatted with J2N Numerics Single or Double ToString methods

data.name: LuceneDev1002_AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Floating point type arithmetic needs to be checked on x86 in .NET Framework.
data.comment: An optional longer localizable description of the diagnostic.

data.name: LuceneDev1002_AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: '{0}' may fail due to floating point precision issues on .NET Framework and .NET Core prior to version 3.0. Floating point type arithmetic needs to be checked on x86 in .NET Framework and may require extra casting.
data.comment: The format-able message the diagnostic displays.

data.name: LuceneDev1002_AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Floating point type arithmetic needs to be checked on some x86 platforms

data.name: LuceneDev1003_AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Method parameters that accept array types should be analyzed to determine whether they are better suited to be ref or out parameters.
data.comment: Java is lacking ref and out parameters. As a result, code ported from Java may use parameters that accept a 1-dimensional array in order to facilitate returning a reference to a value type. In .NET these hacks are unnecessary and these parameters should be converted to ref, out, or ValueTuple parameters.

data.name: LuceneDev1003_AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: '{0}' needs to be analyzed to determine whether the array can be replaced with a ref or out parameter
data.comment: The format-able message the diagnostic displays.

data.name: LuceneDev1003_AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Method parameters that accept array types should be analyzed to determine whether they are better suited to be ref or out parameters

data.name: LuceneDev1004_AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Methods that return array types should be analyzed to determine whether they are better suited to be one or more out parameters or to return a ValueTuple.
data.comment: Java is lacking ref and out parameters. As a result, code ported from Java may use methods that return a 1-dimensional array in order to facilitate returning a reference to multiple value types. In .NET these hacks are unnecessary and these return values should be converted to out parameters or return a ValueTuple.

data.name: LuceneDev1004_AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: '{0}' return type needs to be analyzed to determine whether the array return value can be replaced with one or more out parameters or a return ValueTuple instead of an array to avoid the heap allocation
data.comment: The format-able message the diagnostic displays.

data.name: LuceneDev1004_AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Methods that return array types should be analyzed to determine whether they are better suited to be one or more out parameters or to return a ValueTuple

data.name: LuceneDev1005_AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Types in the Support namespace should not be public

data.name: LuceneDev1005_AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Types in the Lucene.Net.Support namespace should not be public.

data.name: LuceneDev1005_AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: {0} '{1}' should not have public accessibility in the Support namespace

data.name: LuceneDev1006_AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Floating point values embedded in strings should use J2N Numerics formatting

data.name: LuceneDev1006_AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Floating point values should be formatted with J2N.Numerics.Single.ToString() or J2N.Numerics.Double.ToString() before being embedded into strings to ensure consistent behavior across runtimes.
data.comment: An optional longer localizable description of the diagnostic.

data.name: LuceneDev1006_AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: '{0}' may fail due to floating point precision issues on .NET Framework and .NET Core prior to version 3.0. Floating point values should be formatted with J2N.Numerics.Single.ToString() or J2N.Numerics.Double.ToString() before being embedded into strings.
data.comment: The format-able message the diagnostic displays.

data.name: LuceneDev1007_AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Generic Dictionary indexer should not be used to retrieve values (value type)

data.name: LuceneDev1007_AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Reading values from IDictionary<TKey, TValue> or IReadOnlyDictionary<TKey, TValue> via the indexer throws KeyNotFoundException when the key is missing. Use TryGetValue instead.

data.name: LuceneDev1007_AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Generic Dictionary indexer '{0}' may throw KeyNotFoundException. Use TryGetValue instead.

data.name: LuceneDev1008_AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Generic Dictionary indexer should not be used to retrieve values (reference type)

data.name: LuceneDev1008_AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Reading values from IDictionary<TKey, TValue> or IReadOnlyDictionary<TKey, TValue> via the indexer throws KeyNotFoundException when the key is missing. Use TryGetValue instead, and check the out value for null before using it.

data.name: LuceneDev1008_AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Generic Dictionary indexer '{0}' may throw KeyNotFoundException. Use TryGetValue and check the value for null.

data.name: LuceneDev6000_AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Non-generic IDictionary indexer may return null

data.name: LuceneDev6000_AnalyzerDescription
data.value: The non-generic IDictionary indexer returns null for missing keys rather than throwing. Review these usages to ensure the value is checked for null before use.

data.name: LuceneDev6000_AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Non-generic IDictionary indexer '{0}' may return null for missing keys. Verify the value is checked for null before use.

data.name: LuceneDev6001_AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Missing StringComparison argument

data.name: LuceneDev6001_AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Calls to string comparison methods like StartsWith, EndsWith, IndexOf, and LastIndexOf must explicitly specify a StringComparison to enforce culture-invariant and consistent behavior.

data.name: LuceneDev6001_AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Call to '{0}' must specify a StringComparison argument. Use StringComparison.Ordinal or StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase.

data.name: LuceneDev6002_AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Invalid StringComparison argument

data.name: LuceneDev6002_AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Only StringComparison.Ordinal or StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase are allowed to ensure predictable and high-performance string comparisons.

data.name: LuceneDev6002_AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Call to '{0}' uses invalid StringComparison value '{1}'. Only Ordinal or OrdinalIgnoreCase are allowed.

data.name: LuceneDev6003_AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Redundant StringComparison.Ordinal argument

data.name: LuceneDev6003_AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Span-based overloads already perform ordinal comparison by default. Removing redundant arguments simplifies the code and improves clarity.

data.name: LuceneDev6003_AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Call to '{0}' on span overload already uses ordinal comparison. Remove the redundant StringComparison.Ordinal argument.

data.name: LuceneDev6004_AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Invalid StringComparison argument for span overload

data.name: LuceneDev6004_AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Span-based methods only support StringComparison.Ordinal and StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase. Other values are not valid and should be removed or corrected.

data.name: LuceneDev6004_AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Call to '{0}' uses invalid StringComparison value '{1}'. Span overloads only support Ordinal or OrdinalIgnoreCase.

data.name: LuceneDev6005_AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Single-character string argument should use char overload

data.name: LuceneDev6005_AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Using char overloads instead of single-character string literals avoids unnecessary string allocations and improves performance.

data.name: LuceneDev6005_AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Call to '{0}' uses a single-character string literal. Use the char overload instead (e.g., 'x' instead of "x").

data.name: LuceneDev2000_AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Numeric Parse/TryParse should specify an IFormatProvider

data.name: LuceneDev2000_AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Calls to numeric Parse and TryParse methods without an IFormatProvider use the current culture in .NET, but Java Lucene parses numerics using invariant culture. Specify CultureInfo.InvariantCulture (or CultureInfo.CurrentCulture, where intended) explicitly.

data.name: LuceneDev2000_AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Call to '{0}' on numeric type '{1}' should specify an IFormatProvider (typically CultureInfo.InvariantCulture).

data.name: LuceneDev2001_AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Numeric ToString/TryFormat should specify an IFormatProvider

data.name: LuceneDev2001_AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Calls to numeric ToString and TryFormat without an IFormatProvider use the current culture in .NET, but Java Lucene formats numerics using invariant culture. Specify CultureInfo.InvariantCulture (or CultureInfo.CurrentCulture, where intended) explicitly. Calls inside a ToString() override are exempted because that signature has no IFormatProvider parameter.

data.name: LuceneDev2001_AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Call to '{0}' on numeric type '{1}' should specify an IFormatProvider (typically CultureInfo.InvariantCulture).

data.name: LuceneDev2002_AnalyzerTitle
data.value: System.Convert numeric method should specify an IFormatProvider

data.name: LuceneDev2002_AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Calls to System.Convert numeric methods without an IFormatProvider use the current culture in .NET, but Java Lucene uses invariant culture. Specify CultureInfo.InvariantCulture (or CultureInfo.CurrentCulture, where intended) explicitly. Prefer the Parse/TryParse methods on the type for performance and flexibility.

data.name: LuceneDev2002_AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Call to 'Convert.{0}' should specify an IFormatProvider (typically CultureInfo.InvariantCulture).

data.name: LuceneDev2003_AnalyzerTitle
data.value: string.Format with numeric argument should specify an IFormatProvider

data.name: LuceneDev2003_AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Calls to string.Format with numeric arguments and no IFormatProvider format the values in the current culture, but Java Lucene uses invariant culture. Pass CultureInfo.InvariantCulture (or CultureInfo.CurrentCulture, where intended) as the first argument.

data.name: LuceneDev2003_AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Call to 'string.Format' with a numeric argument should specify an IFormatProvider (typically CultureInfo.InvariantCulture).

data.name: LuceneDev2004_AnalyzerTitle
data.value: J2N numeric method should specify an IFormatProvider

data.name: LuceneDev2004_AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Calls to J2N.Numerics.* Parse, TryParse, ToString, or TryFormat methods without an IFormatProvider should be reviewed. J2N intentionally lacks many overloads that omit IFormatProvider; the few that exist (e.g., legacy ToString overloads) should still pass an explicit culture.

data.name: LuceneDev2004_AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Call to '{0}' on J2N numeric type '{1}' should specify an IFormatProvider (typically CultureInfo.InvariantCulture).

data.name: LuceneDev2005_AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Numeric type concatenated to string formats using current culture

data.name: LuceneDev2005_AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Concatenating a numeric value with a string (e.g., "" + i) implicitly formats the value using the current culture, but Java Lucene uses invariant culture. Wrap the numeric expression in .ToString(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture) (or CultureInfo.CurrentCulture, where intended) explicitly.

data.name: LuceneDev2005_AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Numeric value of type '{0}' is concatenated to a string and will be formatted using the current culture. Wrap with .ToString(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture) explicitly.

data.name: LuceneDev2006_AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Numeric value in interpolated string formats using current culture

data.name: LuceneDev2006_AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Interpolated strings (e.g., $"{i}") implicitly format numeric values using the current culture, but Java Lucene uses invariant culture. Use FormattableString.Invariant($"...") or string.Create(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, $"..."), or wrap the value in .ToString(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture) explicitly.

data.name: LuceneDev2006_AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Numeric value of type '{0}' is interpolated into a string and will be formatted using the current culture. Use FormattableString.Invariant or wrap with .ToString(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture) explicitly.

data.name: LuceneDev2007_AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Numeric format/parse uses non-invariant culture

data.name: LuceneDev2007_AnalyzerDescription
data.value: The IFormatProvider passed to a numeric format/parse call is not CultureInfo.InvariantCulture. Java Lucene uses invariant culture by default; suppress this warning explicitly when the use of a different culture is intentional.

data.name: LuceneDev2007_AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Call to '{0}' passes a non-invariant IFormatProvider. Use CultureInfo.InvariantCulture unless current-culture behavior is intentional.

data.name: LuceneDev2008_AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Numeric format/parse uses CultureInfo.InvariantCulture (review aid)

data.name: LuceneDev2008_AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Disabled by default. Enable during the culture-correctness audit to walk every numeric format/parse call that already passes CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, so each can be verified.

data.name: LuceneDev2008_AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Call to '{0}' passes CultureInfo.InvariantCulture; verify this matches Lucene's behavior at this site.
